strengths: Homepage has clear H1 and H2 headings that describe the product.
Documentation pages use numbered step lists, which are highly extractable.
Several FAQ-style pages with concise question-answer pairs.
weaknesses: Integration pages duplicate content patterns with little variation.
No structured data (JSON-LD) found on blog posts.
recommendations: Add JSON-LD Article markup to blog posts at https://composio.dev/blog/.
Add unique meta descriptions to all /integrations/* pages.

strengths: Company name 'Composio' is consistently used across the site.
Founder names appear in About page with roles.
weaknesses: No Organization JSON-LD schema found on the homepage.
Backlink profile not directly assessable from on-site evidence.
Product entity lacks a Wikidata or schema.org/Product reference.
recommendations: Add Organization JSON-LD to https://composio.dev/ with name, url, logo, sameAs.
Add Product JSON-LD to the main product landing page.

strengths: robots.txt exists at https://composio.dev/robots.txt and allows all crawlers.
Canonical tags present on sampled pages.
recommendations: Create https://composio.dev/llms.txt to improve LLM discoverability.
Expand sitemap.xml to include /changelog/* and /blog/* pages.

strengths: Comparison pages (e.g. vs LangChain) provide citable factual claims.
Integration count claims ('3000+ integrations') are prominent and specific.
Pricing page uses concrete tier names and limits.
weaknesses: Most statistics lack source citations or footnotes.
No dedicated press or media kit page found.
recommendations: Add source citations to statistics and comparison claims.
Create a press kit page at https://composio.dev/press/.

evidence.summary: Homepage. Clear H1: 'AI Integrations Platform'. No JSON-LD found. Meta description present.
Comparison article. Citable claims about feature differences. No JSON-LD.

report_markdown:
# GEO Audit: composio.dev

**Overall Score: 68 / 100**

## Summary

composio.dev scores well on content extractability and citation-worthiness but has clear gaps
in technical LLM accessibility (no llms.txt) and entity authority (missing JSON-LD).

## Lever 1 — Content Structure and Extractability (18/25)

The site uses clear headings and FAQ patterns. Integration pages are repetitive.

## Lever 2 — Entity Authority (15/25)

Brand consistency is good but structured entity data is absent.

## Lever 3 — Technical Accessibility (17/25)

robots.txt and sitemap are healthy. llms.txt is missing.

## Lever 4 — Citation-Worthiness (18/25)

Comparison and pricing pages provide strong citable claims but lack source references.

## Top Recommendations

1. Add llms.txt to improve LLM discoverability.
2. Add Organization and Product JSON-LD to key pages.
3. Add source citations to statistical claims.
